title: Julien Marcel
text: Home * People * Julien Marcel Julien Marcel , a French jurist, professor of law, and avocational computer chess programmer. He made first chess programming experiences in the early 90s, when he wrote a little chess program in Basic for the Commodore Amiga 500 , applying the Minimax principle none recursively . Years later, he learned about Alpha-Beta from Paul Verhelst's chess programming pages, and in summer 2007 started to work on his UCI compatible engine Prédateur , written in Pascal using the Lazarus IDE along with its Free Pascal compiler [1] . Julien is active poster in Computer Chess Forums and served as moderator in CCC [2] .
